task_completion.description: Locate the user account associated with the email domain '@external-vendor.net'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 2N Access Commander 'Users' section with a search for '@external-vendor.net' or the profile of 'Alex Ghost' (admin@external-vendor.net) opened.

task_completion.description: Save the assigned RFID card number to /home/ga/compromised_card.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal or text editor where an RFID card number (matching the one seen in the user's profile) is being saved to the file path '/home/ga/compromised_card.txt'.

task_completion.description: Revoke physical access by removing the RFID card credential from the user's profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 2N Access Commander GUI showing the RFID card being deleted or the card field being empty for the target user.
Screenshots of the user's profile in the GUI showing the 'Company' text field updated to exactly 'Quarantined'.

task_completion.description: Save the modifications without deleting the user account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent clicking a 'Save' or 'Apply' button in the user profile, or a success notification indicating the profile was updated, with the user account still visible in the system.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the 2N Access Commander web interface to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the web browser navigating the 2N Access Commander UI to remove the card and change the company, rather than using curl/API commands in the terminal.

integrity.description: The extracted RFID card number matches the actual randomized card assigned to the user.
integrity.visual_evidence: The number saved in compromised_card.txt must visually match the RFID number shown in the user's profile before it was deleted. It should not be a placeholder or guessed value.

integrity.description: The user account was modified, not deleted.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent clicking 'Delete User' or confirming a user deletion. The final screens should show the user profile still exists but with updated fields.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target user is 'Alex Ghost' with the email 'admin@external-vendor.net'. The RFID card number is randomized per session, so you must verify that the number saved to /home/ga/compromised_card.txt matches the exact number displayed in Alex Ghost's profile before it was removed. The expected company status is exactly 'Quarantined'.
